skill.name: conductor-manage
skill.description: Manage Conductor track lifecycle tasks, including listing, archiving, restoring, renaming, deleting, and cleanup.
skill.summary: Coordinate Conductor track maintenance with explicit checks and confirmations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found the high-risk matches are mostly markdown syntax and workflow examples. The real concern is that this skill guides agents through local file moves, commits, cleanup, and permanent deletion, so it should publish with a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Filesystem Operations in Workflow Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to remove, move, and rewrite Conductor track directories. It includes confirmation steps, but the operations can permanently delete local project files if a track ID is wrong or unsafe.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The playbook explicitly documents permanent deletion and shell removal commands. The same file also requires confirmation, so this is a legitimate but elevated operational risk rather than malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Incomplete Track ID Validation for Path-Based Operations
medium_findings.description: The rename flow validates the new ID format, but archive, restore, and delete flows mainly check existence before constructing paths. Human review should ensure track IDs are normalized and constrained before file operations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed instructions show format validation for new rename IDs, but not equivalent validation for all track IDs used in filesystem paths. The risk depends on the agent implementation and repository state.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern Matches Are Mostly Markdown Syntax
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many Ruby or shell backtick and weak crypto patterns. Reviewed examples are markdown inline code, command examples, placeholders, and headings, not executable Ruby code or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown documentation and argument tables. No executable Ruby backtick usage or weak cryptographic function was found in the reviewed skill files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples Affect Project Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The playbook includes shell examples for mkdir, mv, rm, git add, and git commit. These are expected for track management, but agents should execute them only after validating paths and user confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present in fenced bash examples and operate on local repository paths. They are not hidden or networked, but they can modify or delete files.

content.value_statement: Conductor track maintenance can become inconsistent when archive, restore, delete, and cleanup steps are manual. This skill provides structured workflows with checks, confirmations, and registry updates.
actual_capabilities: Lists active, completed, and archived Conductor tracks from registry and track folders.
Archives completed tracks into the Conductor archive directory with metadata updates.
Restores archived tracks and updates the central track registry.
Deletes tracks only after an explicit permanent deletion confirmation flow.
Renames track IDs and updates related metadata, plan, and registry references.
Detects orphaned directories, missing registry entries, incomplete tracks, and stale work.
limitations: Requires an existing Conductor repository structure before use.
Can modify or delete local project files when destructive modes are approved.
Does not replace project-specific review of track IDs, paths, or cleanup results.
Git commits may fail if the repository has conflicts, hooks, or permission issues.

use_cases.title: Clean Track Registry
use_cases.description: Find and repair mismatches between tracks.md and the conductor/tracks directory.

use_cases.title: Archive Completed Work
use_cases.description: Move completed tracks into an archive while preserving metadata and registry history.

use_cases.title: Recover Archived Tracks
use_cases.description: Restore a track from the archive when work needs to resume or be reviewed.

prompt_templates.title: List Tracks
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner overview of current track status.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-manage to list all Conductor tracks and summarize active, completed, and archived counts.

prompt_templates.title: Archive One Track
prompt_templates.scenario: Archive a completed track with normal safeguards.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-manage to archive the completed track named TRACK_ID. Verify its status and ask for confirmation before changing files.

prompt_templates.title: Restore From Archive
prompt_templates.scenario: Bring an archived track back into active tracking.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-manage to restore TRACK_ID from the archive. Check for path conflicts and update tracks.md and metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Full Cleanup Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced maintenance across registry and directories.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-manage cleanup mode to inspect orphaned directories, registry orphans, incomplete tracks, and stale in-progress tracks. Present changes before applying them.

output_examples.output: A grouped status report showing active, completed, and archived tracks with counts, IDs, progress, and last update dates.
A confirmation summary that names the track, archive reason, planned file moves, registry update, and commit message before execution.
A cleanup report listing orphaned directories, registry orphans, incomplete tracks, stale tracks, and selectable repair actions.

best_practices: Review the listed file changes before approving archive, restore, rename, delete, or cleanup actions.
Back up important track data before deleting any track directory.
Validate track IDs and repository state before allowing shell commands to run.
anti_patterns: Do not run delete or cleanup automatically in an unreviewed repository.
Do not use this skill outside a repository that has a valid conductor directory.
Do not ignore git errors after files have been moved or registry entries changed.

faq.question: Does this skill create new Conductor tracks?
faq.answer: No. It manages existing tracks through list, archive, restore, delete, rename, and cleanup workflows.

faq.question: Can it permanently delete files?
faq.answer: Yes. Delete mode removes track directories after an explicit confirmation step.

faq.question: Does it make git commits?
faq.answer: The playbook recommends commits after archive, restore, rename, delete, and cleanup operations.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it guides local filesystem changes and deletion.

faq.question: What tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users check before running cleanup?
faq.answer: Users should confirm the Conductor structure, review detected issues, and approve each repair action.
